description:
You are acting as an on-call network engineer who has just received a ticket that reports high latency on the primary interface “eth0”.  
For a first-level performance optimisation you have decided to push the interface MTU from 1500 to 9000 (jumbo-frames) and keep a minimal audit trail.

Your workstation already contains:

1. Directory  
   /home/user/netops/

2. File that represents the current interface settings  
   /home/user/netops/interfaces.cfg  
   Current exact contents (3 lines, no trailing spaces):  
   interface=eth0  
   mtu=1500  
   state=up  

Your job (do NOT show the commands you use, only perform them):

A. Inside /home/user/netops/ create a text file called optimisation.log that contains two clearly separated blocks, **Before** and **After**, exactly in the following fixed, testable format (each “⇒” and colon is literal, capitalisation must match, no extra whitespace):

----
Before
interface⇒eth0
mtu⇒1500
state⇒up

After
interface⇒eth0
mtu⇒9000
state⇒up
----

B. Atomically (i.e., by the end of the task) edit /home/user/netops/interfaces.cfg so that the mtu line now reads  
mtu=9000  
and nothing else in that file is altered.

Verification rules the grader will apply:

1. /home/user/netops/optimisation.log must exist and be world-readable (-rw-r--r-- or more permissive).  
2. The log must contain exactly the eight non-empty lines shown above (separator blank lines in the same positions).  
3. /home/user/netops/interfaces.cfg must now contain the three lines shown in the “After” block, in the same order.  
4. No other files should be modified or created anywhere in the filesystem.

Hints you might find useful (do NOT reveal your concrete commands):  
 • A couple of echo and redirection operations are sufficient.  
 • Make sure to preserve the original order of keys (interface, mtu, state).  
 • Permissions can be set in one step after the log is written.

The whole procedure can be completed with only two or three shell commands.
truth:
Initial state before agent starts:

Directory:
/home/user/netops/      (already exists, permissions drwxr-xr-x)

File:
/home/user/netops/interfaces.cfg
Contents (exact):
interface=eth0
mtu=1500
state=up

No other files inside /home/user/netops/.

Expected final state for automated tests:

1. /home/user/netops/interfaces.cfg
   Contents (exact):
interface=eth0
mtu=9000
state=up

2. /home/user/netops/optimisation.log
   Permissions: world-readable (at least 644)
   Contents (exact, including blank lines):

Before
interface⇒eth0
mtu⇒1500
state⇒up

After
interface⇒eth0
mtu⇒9000
state⇒up

No trailing spaces on any line; file ends with a single newline.  
No other filesystem changes.
